filename: Adding a chemical which does not exist in unit to chemical inventory.docx
relative_path: Waste management/CHEMICAL INVENTORY HANDLING INSTRUCTIONS OCT.2018/Adding a chemical which does not exist in unit to chemical inventory.docx
full_text:
Adding a chemical which does not exist in unit to chemical inventory

Choose Browse from home page.

Choose the correct location from tree structure on the left.

Choose New Container from menu bar on the top.

Choose Select Substance from opening window.

In Select databases to search choose both ”Inventory Enterprise” = chemical inventory of university and ”ChemACX” = external database linked to chemical inventory.

It is advisable to search by CAS number. Substance name can be used as well.

Choose the correct search result by clicking Details (in center above the molecular structure) and OK. After that you will be taking back to New Container window you opened before.

Type in Number of Copies number of copies you want to add.

Change at least the following fields: Container Type, Unit of Measure, Container size and Initial Amount. If you use own abbreviation, unselect ”Autogenerate Container ID” and type identification code into Container ID field. After that click OK.

In window with text “New Container has been created” click again OK.

If you added multiple containers and use own abbreviation, you need to edit identification code for every added container (Update Edit container).
